items.label: Full Development
items.description: From PRD to deploy, weekly iterations

methodology.subtitle: Six clear phases that take an AI or Full-Stack project from initial idea to a system running in production. The same approach works for organizations, development teams, and founders — adapted to scope and budget.

phases.title: Initial Consultation
phases.subtitle: Half an hour. No cost. No commitment.
phases.paragraph: We meet for thirty minutes on Zoom or by phone to understand what you want to build, the business pain behind the request, and your timeline. I'll give an honest read on whether the project is a fit — sometimes I'll refer you elsewhere if it isn't my domain. If there's a fit, I leave the call with a first-pass picture of scope, top risks, and a rough price range. If there isn't, we lost 30 minutes and nothing more.
deliverables: Clear understanding of business and technical goals
Honest recommendation — take it on or refer out

phases.title: Specification Document
phases.subtitle: A detailed PRD with mockups and architecture — so we're all talking about the same thing.
phases.paragraph: This is where the idea becomes a working document. I write a PRD (Product Requirements Document) covering all features, user flows, edge cases, and integrations. In parallel I build mockups of the main screens (using tools like Stitch or v0) and propose a fitted technical architecture — which database, which services, how AI plugs in if relevant. By the end of this phase you have a 10–30 page document you can show to a board, a development team, or a partner — and I have a roadmap to build from.
deliverables: Detailed PRD with feature list and user stories

phases.title: Weekly Iteration Development
phases.subtitle: Weekly sprint, Friday demo, fast iteration.
phases.paragraph: The core of the work. I run weekly sprints — on Sunday we decide what goes into the round, on Thursday or Friday there's a demo of what shipped, and any feedback gets handled in the next round. You see real progress every week, not a month of silence followed by a big reveal. I work in Git with feature branches, clear commit messages, and documented PRs — so you have full visibility even when we're not in a meeting. Every commit passes type-check and lint, and I use AI tooling (Claude Code, Copilot) as a productivity multiplier — but the code itself goes through human review.
deliverables: Live PROGRESS.md updates in the project

phases.title: Testing & Refinement
phases.subtitle: QA, accessibility, performance — before going live.
phases.paragraph: Before the system sees the light of day, we run a structured testing pass. Functional tests for every critical user flow, accessibility tests against IS 5568 / WCAG 2.1 (especially important for public-sector and enterprise projects), performance testing with Lighthouse and Core Web Vitals, and basic security checks (OWASP Top 10). Load testing if relevant. Every bug found goes into the list, gets fixed, and gets retested. This phase is short on time but critical to quality.
deliverables: QA report covering every user flow tested

phases.title: Launch & Support
phases.subtitle: Deploy, monitoring, and 30 days of included support.
phases.paragraph: Launch day. I handle the full deployment — Vercel, Railway, a Hetzner VPS, or your internal cloud. I set up monitoring (Sentry for errors, Plausible/PostHog for analytics if relevant), confirm DNS, SSL, and the rest of the infrastructure are working, and run a final smoke test in production. After launch, you get 30 days of included support — every bug, every question, every small fix is handled at no extra charge. This is when the project really moves from "code" to "a live system".

phases.title: Ongoing Maintenance
phases.subtitle: Monthly retainer — optional, never required.
phases.paragraph: After the included 30 days, if you want me to keep maintaining the system, I offer a flexible monthly retainer. The idea is straightforward: a guaranteed number of hours per month for bug fixes, security updates, small features, and fast incident response. You can also choose to take the code in-house and work with your own team — I'll run a clean handover and leave thorough documentation. No lock-in. My approach is to build systems that can run without me.
deliverables: Option to add new features at your pace

principles.title: Four Principles That Run Through Every Phase
principles.subtitle: Why I work this way — and how much it saves you down the line

items.title: Full Transparency
items.description: Git access, weekly demos, live PROGRESS.md updates. No surprises.

items.title: Short Iterations
items.description: Weekly sprints. If something isn't working, we change it next round — not next month.

items.title: No Lock-in
items.description: Your code, your infrastructure, your documentation. You can always continue with an internal team.

items.title: AI as Multiplier, Not Replacement
items.description: I use Claude Code and AI tooling to accelerate development, but every line of code goes through human review.

faq.title: Ask Me Anything
faq.subtitle: Common questions about my services, process, and expertise

questions.technologies: What technologies do you work with?
questions.availability: Are you currently available for new projects?
questions.process: What does your development process look like?
questions.aiExpertise: Tell me about your AI expertise
questions.hebrew: Do you support Hebrew and RTL development?
questions.contact: How can I get in touch with you?
questions.timeline: How long does a typical project take?

answers.services: I offer five core services: AI automation and chatbot development, full-stack web applications (Node.js/Next.js/React), EdTech platforms, tech workshops for teams and individuals, and WhatsApp automation systems. Every project gets clean code, bilingual support, and production-ready delivery.
answers.pricing: Pricing depends on scope and complexity. Small landing pages or tools start around ₪3,000–6,000. Full-stack web apps with backend and database range from ₪8,000–25,000. AI integrations and automation systems typically run ₪5,000–20,000. I'm happy to discuss your specific needs and provide a custom estimate.
answers.technologies: My core stack is Node.js, Next.js, React, and TypeScript for the backend and frontend. I use PostgreSQL and Supabase for databases, Prisma as an ORM, and Framer Motion for UI animations. For AI, I work with the Claude API, OpenAI, Whisper, ElevenLabs, and multi-agent systems with MCP.
answers.availability: Yes, I'm currently available for new projects! I typically take on 1–2 projects at a time to ensure quality and responsiveness. Response time is usually within a few hours. The best way to check is to reach out directly via WhatsApp or email.
answers.process: My process has four phases: Discover (understanding your goals and users), Plan (architecture and tech stack decisions), Build with regular check-ins, and Launch with support. I use agile iterations with demos so you always know what's happening. Clear communication at every step.
answers.aiExpertise: I've built AI-powered systems across several domains: voice conversation apps using Whisper and ElevenLabs, Hebrew NLP with RAG pipelines, children's story generation with content filtering, multi-agent automation systems, and Claude Code integrations. I also maintain the /claude-code and /guide pages — 14 hands-on guides covering agents and infrastructure for developers learning AI tooling.
answers.hebrew: Absolutely — Hebrew and RTL are a core specialty. I build bilingual (HE/EN) applications with proper RTL layout, logical CSS properties, and full i18n support using next-intl. I have deep experience with Hebrew typography, keyboard layouts, and the nuances of right-to-left UI development.
answers.contact: You can reach me on WhatsApp at 052-542-7474 (fastest response), by email at eladhiteclearning@gmail.com, or via the contact form on this site. I usually respond within a few hours during business days.
answers.timeline: Timeline depends on scope. A landing page or simple tool: 1–2 weeks. A full web application with backend and database: 4–8 weeks. Complex AI systems or large platforms: 2–4 months. I'll give you a realistic estimate upfront — no surprises.
answers.support: Yes! I offer post-launch support and maintenance. After delivery, I typically provide 2–4 weeks of included fixes. For ongoing maintenance, I offer monthly retainer plans. I also write documentation so you can manage the system yourself if you prefer.
